hero.name: Abdirahiim jamal abdullahi
hero.subtitle: Building scalable infrastructure and full-stack applications with modern cloud-native technologies.

about.bio: I'm a passionate Cloud & DevOps engineer with hands-on experience in AWS, containerization, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure-as-code. I love building things that are reliable, scalable, and well-architected. This portfolio itself is a living example — deployed across multiple AWS strategies (ECS, EKS, Serverless) with full Terraform IaC and GitHub Actions CI/CD.

experience.description: Designing and implementing AWS infrastructure using Terraform. Managing EKS clusters, CI/CD pipelines, and monitoring solutions.
Assisted in migrating on-premise services to AWS. Dockerized microservices and set up ECS Fargate with CI/CD pipelines.

projects.title: Cloud Portfolio
projects.description: A full-stack portfolio deployed across ECS Fargate, EKS, and Serverless architectures. Infrastructure managed entirely with Terraform with automated CI/CD via GitHub Actions.

projects.title: Kubernetes Cluster Setup
projects.description: Production-grade EKS cluster with node groups, IAM policies, ingress controllers, and monitoring stack (Prometheus + Grafana).

projects.title: Serverless API
projects.description: Fully serverless REST API using Lambda, API Gateway, and DynamoDB with Infrastructure-as-Code in Terraform.

blog.title: Deploying a React App on AWS ECS Fargate
blog.excerpt: A step-by-step guide to containerizing and deploying a React application on AWS ECS Fargate with Terraform.
blog.content:
In this post, I'll walk through the process of deploying a React application on AWS ECS Fargate using Terraform for infrastructure management...

## Prerequisites
- An AWS account
- Docker installed
- Terraform installed

## Step 1: Containerize the App
First, create a multi-stage Dockerfile for your React app...

## Step 2: Write Terraform Config
Define your ECS cluster, task definition, and service...

## Step 3: Deploy
Run terraform apply and watch your app come to life!
